encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Updating toward the encoding template v03

div.p:
* svasti ŇõarabhapurńĀd vikkramopanatasńĀmantamakuŠĻ≠acŇęŠłćńĀmaŠĻáiprabhńĀpra
sekńĀmbudhautapńĀdayugalo ripuvilńĀsinńęsńęmantoddharaŠĻáahetur vvasu
vasudhńĀgopradaŠłę paramabhńĀgavato mńĀtńĀpitŠĻõpńĀdńĀnuddhyńĀtaŇõ ŇõrńęmahńĀsude
varńĀjaŠł• tuŠĻáŠłćarakabhuktńęyacullaŠĻáŠłćarake prativńĀsikuŠĻ≠umbinas sa
mńĀj√ĪńĀpayati

viditam astu vo yathńĀyaŠĻĀ grńĀmaŠł• tŠĻõitridaŇõapatisadanasukha
pratiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀkaro yńĀvad raviŇõaŇõitńĀrńĀkiraŠĻáapratihataghorńĀndhakńĀraŠĻĀ ja
gad avatiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hate tńĀvad upabhogyas sanidhis sopanidhir acńĀŠĻ≠abhaŠĻ≠aprńĀveŇõyas
sarvvakaravisarjjitaŠł• rńĀjyamahńĀdevńęrońĀjakulaiŠł• mńĀtńĀpitror ńĀtmanaŠĻĀŇõ ca pu
ŠĻáyńĀbhivŠĻõddhaye UdakapŇęrvvaŠĻĀ koauŇõikasagotratŠĻõitrisahasravidyabhńĀskarasvńĀmi
prabhńĀkarasvńĀmibarbbarisvńĀmiboŠĻ≠asvńĀmidattasvńĀmiviŠĻ£ŠĻáusvńĀmi.
phalgusvńĀmisvńĀmikńęrttisvńĀmiŇõaŠĻĀŠĻÖkarasvńĀmimńĀŠĻĀ tńĀmbramraŇõńĀsanenńĀtisŠĻõ
ŠĻ£ŠĻ≠o bhŇętvńĀsmńĀbhir anumoditaŠł•

te yŇęyam evam upalaŠĻĀbhyaiŠĻ£ńĀm ńĀj√ĪńĀŇõrava
ŠĻáavidheyńĀ bhŇętvńĀ yathocitaŠĻĀ bhogabhńĀgam upanayantas sukhaŠĻĀ prativatsyatha
bhaviŠĻ£yataŇõ ca bhŇęmipńĀn anudarŇõayati.

lg.l: dattńĀŠĻĀ bhuvaŠĻĀ bhavatu vo matir eva goptuŠĻĀ.

div.p:
tad bhavadbhi
r apy eŠĻ£ńĀ dattir anupńĀlayitavyńĀ vyńĀsagńętńĀŠĻĀŇõ cńĀtra ŇõlokńĀn udńĀhara
nti.

app.note: The shape of the vowel marker for au is slightly irregular, but is the same as in other copperplate charters of the kings of Ňöarabhapura.

Traces of the vowel marker for i are also present, in addition to the vowel marker for ŠĻõ. It seems that tŠĻõi was engraved first, by accident, and it was corrected in tŠĻõ by partly chiselling out i.

div.p: Success! Hail! From Ňöarabhapura, the illustrious great SudevarńĀja whose feet are washed by the sprinkling water that is the brilliance of the crest-jewels on the crests of his feudatories who have been brought into submission by his prowess, who made the women of his enemies tear out their parted hair, who is a giver of riches, land, and cattle, who is entirely devoted to ViŠĻ£ŠĻáu, and favoured by his father and mother, issues the following command to the landholders settled in the village of CullaŠĻáŠłćaraka situated in the TuŠĻáŠłćaraka bhukti:

Let it be known to you that by means of this copperplate charter and preceded by a libation of water, this village has been granted on behalf of the chief queen and the royal family and confirmed by us, for the increase of religious merit of father, mother, and ourselves, to trisahasravidya BhńĀskarasvńĀmin, PrabhńĀkarasvńĀmin, BarbbarisvńĀmin, BoŠĻ≠asvńĀmin, DattasvńĀmin, SvńĀmin, ViŠĻ£ŠĻáusvńĀmin, PhalgusvńĀmin, KńęrttisvńĀmin, and ŇöaŠĻÖkarasvńĀmin, all belonging to the KauŇõika gotra. The grant of the village, which leads to happiness and fame in the abode of the lord of the gods i.e., Indra‚Äôs heaven, is to be enjoyed as long as the world will endure, the world of which the darkness of the night is removed by the rays of the sun, moon and stars; comes along with the right to hidden treasures and deposits; is not to be entered by irregular or regular troops; and is exempt from all taxes.

Having taken note of that, you should be obedient to the command received from themn i.e., the donees and render the proper shares of periodical offerings and produce, while living happily in this village. And the king instructs the future rulers:

The ancients, reflecting on virtue, proclaim that the religious merit arising from the protection of a grant is more excellent than that which arises from the giving itself; therefore, your intent should indeed be to protect the earth that has been granted to a Brahmin of perfectly pure family and religious learning.
This gift is to be protected by you as well! And in this respect they quote the following stanzas sung by VyńĀsa:
Gold is the first child of Agni, land is born of ViŠĻ£ŠĻáu, and cows are the offspring of SŇęrya: Therefore, whoever would give gold, a cow, and land would give the three worlds.
This copperplate charter is incomplete: the third (and last) plate as well as the seal are missing. Hence, the regnal year in which the command was issued is unknown.
First edited by Hira Lal from the original plates; published again by Shastri 50-52; re-edited here by Natasja Bosma based on the published photographs.
